subject: [PATCH v3 00/10] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer Miklos Szeredi expressed uncertainty about the buffer passing approach, noting that it simplifies logic at multiple layers but may limit dynamic capacity addition, and requested input from Joanne Koong on her preference.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, 5 Jun 2026 at 00:07, Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com> wrote:

> In my opinion, passing in the buffers contiguously as a pool leads to
> simpler and cleaner logic at the fuse, io-uring, and userspace layers.
> If a daemon wants to dynamically add/remove buffer capacity in the
> future, they can add/remove another buffer pool or another ring,
> depending on their workload.
>
> From Bernd's perspective, passing in each buffer individually allows
> daemons who want to dynamically add/remove buffer capacity in the
> future to add/remove capacity one buffer at a time based on load
> instead of needing to commit upfront to adding/removing a pool.
>
> Could you please share your preference? This series is targeting the
> quickly approaching 7.2 merge window.

I simply lack context to make a good decision right now.  I can dive
into this next week...

Thanks,
Miklos

2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch series adds buffer pools and zero-copy functionality to the fuse io-uring implementation, allowing servers to pass in a managed buffer pool that reduces memory usage requirements and enables future optimizations. It also allows for direct access to client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer, requiring CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges. The changes are based on registered buffers and zero-copy, which can provide significant throughput improvements depending on the server's per-request latency and backing I/O characteristics.
